response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with 15+ years of experience, strong testing culture, and relevant systems-level exposure (low-latency DLang services, async I/O, event-driven architectures). However, the role centers on hands-on modern C++ with STL, multithreading, message brokers, MQTT, and IEC 62443 secure development for industrial automation — areas where the resume shows only brief, dated C++ experience (a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015) and no industrial-cybersecurity or MQTT/OT-protocol background. His recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js web and product work, making him a poor fit for a senior C++ industrial-platform role despite his general seniority.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, backend, desktop, and embedded contexts
Systems-level performance experience: low-latency, async I/O microservices handling millions of requests/sec (DLang)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: Minimal and outdated hands-on C++ experience (short 2014–2015 role); recent work is TypeScript/Node.js
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, or OT protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP3)
No demonstrated message-broker-based large-scale C++ system design or Azure cloud experience

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) or MQTT/OT background
Strong general seniority and performance-systems experience
